title: Fix 'Failed to load' error across Poetry, Dua, and Songs sections

requirements.text: Audit the backend Motoko actor in backend/main.mo and verify that getAllPoetry, getAllDua, and getAllSongs query functions have correct function signatures, return types, and stable variable access. Ensure each function returns an Array of the correct record type with all expected fields (id, title, content, category, likeCount, and any media blob fields) without trapping or rejecting. Fix any issues found so all three query functions execute successfully.
acceptanceCriteria: getAllPoetry query can be invoked and returns an array without trapping
getAllDua query can be invoked and returns an array without trapping
getAllSongs query can be invoked and returns an array without trapping
All returned records include the expected fields: id, title, content, category, likeCount, and any media blob fields

requirements.text: Fix the useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs React Query hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts. Ensure each hook calls the exact backend actor method name as defined in the Motoko actor, uses a stable query key, and correctly transforms the backend Candid response by mapping Motoko record field names to the matching TypeScript type field names. Add try/catch error handling so a failed fetch returns a user-friendly error state instead of crashing the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: useGetAllPoetry calls the correct Motoko method name and maps Candid fields to TypeScript types
useGetAllDua calls the correct Motoko method name and maps Candid fields to TypeScript types
useGetAllSongs calls the correct Motoko method name and maps Candid fields to TypeScript types
A network or canister error sets isError=true and does not throw an unhandled exception
Each hook uses a stable, unique query key

requirements.text: Verify and fix PoetryPage, DuaPage, and SongsPage components so their loading, error, and empty states are triggered correctly based on the actual data shapes returned by the fixed hooks. Remove any field-name mismatches or incorrect type assumptions that cause a valid response to be rendered as an error state. Ensure content renders when the backend returns valid data.
acceptanceCriteria: PoetryPage displays a loading skeleton while fetching, shows error message on failure, and renders post cards on success
DuaPage displays a loading skeleton while fetching, shows error message on failure, and renders post cards on success
SongsPage displays a loading skeleton while fetching, shows error message on failure, and renders post cards on success
No valid backend response is incorrectly treated as an error due to field-name mismatches

requirements.text: Fix the MixPage component to correctly aggregate data from the useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs hooks after the hook fixes in REQ-42. Ensure the combined trending feed renders content from all three categories when any category has available posts, and shows appropriate loading/error states.
acceptanceCriteria: MixPage shows a loading state while any of the three data sources are loading
MixPage renders a unified feed of posts sorted by like count when data is available
MixPage shows an error state only when all three data sources fail

constraints: Do not change the PIN value for the admin panel (09186114)
Do not modify files listed under frontend.immutablePaths
Keep all Motoko logic in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture)
nonGoals: Adding new content categories or post types
Changing the app visual design or branding
Modifying the hamburger menu, audio filters, or IndexedDB features
Altering the admin upload forms or content management interface
